verdict_reasoning: FN1 is a secreted extracellular matrix protein with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, lipidation, or covalent surface tethering. Although it transiently binds cell-surface integrin receptors (α5β1, αVβ3) during matrix assembly and fibrillogenesis, these are reversible non-covalent interactions in equilibrium with the soluble pool. The protein lacks its own membrane anchor; the recruiters (integrins) are the surface targets, not the fibronectin body. This transient equilibrium interaction does not confer stable surface accessibility. No cell-state-induced ecto-display, tissue-restricted surface pool, lysosomal exocytosis, dual-localization PM cycling, or stable post-translational TM-partner tethering is documented.
